may be intentional, since it is usually asked, "Have you read
Shakespeare ?" meaning his 'works.' In Sanskrit too such a practice
is prevalent. For, Magha is identified with his SiSupalavadha by

bdhu a Jain astrologer, is said to have written a Sarhhitd entitled
3
the Bhddrabdhavi Sarhhitd and a commentary on the S&ryaprajftapti.
(viii) Bhaljotpala*s extensive quotations from the author's
Samdsasamhiid have saved that work from total oblivion. For, we
have no other means of getting at its contents than through these
references, though incomplete. Altogether 122J verses of this
abridged Sarhhitd are quoted in his Vivrti. That work must have
been lost soon after Utpala's time, since Al-Blruni says nothing
about it.
